name: 李显龙
current_positions: Member of the Parliament of Singapore

education.institution: United States Army Command and General Staff College
John F. Kennedy School of Government, Harvard University

work_experience.position: Member of Parliament (Teck Ghee SMC, then Ang Mo Kio GRC)

person_relationships.description: Married since 1985; Ho Ching is former CEO of Temasek Holdings and current Chairperson of Temasek Trust
Younger brother; former CEO of Singtel; public family feud since 2017 over Lee Kuan Yew's house at 38 Oxley Road
Younger sister (1955-2024); neurologist and former director of National Neuroscience Institute; estranged over Oxley Road dispute
Succeeded Lee as Prime Minister in May 2024 and as PAP Secretary-General in December 2024
Chan led the PAP team in Tanjong Pagar GRC after Lee Kuan Yew's death in 2015; served in multiple cabinet positions under LHL

family_members.name: Kwa Geok Choo
family_members.industry_or_organization: Lawyer; Co-founder of Lee & Lee law firm

family_members.name: Ho Ching
family_members.industry_or_organization: Former CEO of Temasek Holdings; Chairperson of Temasek Trust

family_members.name: Li Xiuqi
family_members.industry_or_organization: Daughter from first marriage to Wong Ming Yang

family_members.name: Li Yipeng
family_members.industry_or_organization: Son from first marriage to Wong Ming Yang

family_members.name: Li Hongyi
family_members.industry_or_organization: Son with Ho Ching; Deputy Director at Government Technology Agency of Singapore

family_members.name: Lee Wei Ling
family_members.industry_or_organization: Neurologist; former director of National Neuroscience Institute (d. 2024)

political_stances.stance_content: Advocated pragmatic, consensus-based governance with continuity. Maintained PAP's dominant-party system while introducing limited political reforms.
Publicly addressed the Oxley Road family dispute in Parliament, stating his siblings' accusations of abusing his power were baseless and that the government would handle the matter transparently.
Led Singapore's COVID-19 response with circuit breaker measures and graduated reopening, balancing public health and economic concerns.
Handed over power to Lawrence Wong's 4G team, emphasizing the importance of orderly leadership renewal in Singapore.
Maintained Singapore's open economy model, pursued free trade agreements, and positioned Singapore as a global financial hub.
Advocated principles-based foreign policy: upholding international law, freedom of navigation, and ASEAN centrality.

major_achievements.achievement: Became the 3rd Prime Minister of Singapore
Served as Prime Minister for 20 years, overseeing economic growth and transformation
Led PAP to victory in 5 general elections (2006, 2011, 2015, 2020, with transition in 2024)
Oversaw orderly transition of power to Lawrence Wong, Singapore's 4th Prime Minister

biography_summary: Lee Hsien Loong is a Singaporean politician and former military officer who served as the 3rd Prime Minister of Singapore (2004-2024) and is now Senior Minister. He is the eldest son of founding PM Lee Kuan Yew and Kwa Geok Choo. Educated at Cambridge and Harvard, he entered politics in 1984 and rose to become DPM (1990-2004) before becoming PM. He was married first to Wong Ming Yang (who died in 1982), with whom he had two children, and then to Ho Ching (former Temasek CEO). His siblings Lee Hsien Yang and Lee Wei Ling were estranged since 2017 over a dispute about their father's house at 38 Oxley Road. He led Singapore through the COVID-19 pandemic and oversaw the transition to PM Lawrence Wong in 2024.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with comprehensive family members (parents, siblings, children, spouses), political stances, person relationships including Oxley Road family dispute, major achievements from Wikipedia and web search
